title: Topsoeâ€™s Advanced Technology Selected for a Sustainable Aviation Fuel and Renewable Diesel Project in Uruguay
content: Topsoe has been selected by ANCAP, Uruguayâ€™s state-owned energy company, to deliver technology and services for a new sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel plant at the La Teja Refinery in Montevideo. Under the agreement, Topsoe will provide its HydroFlexÂ® technology, proprietary equipment, and catalysts for the facility's hydroprocessed esters and fatty acids (HEFA) unit. Designed to process rapeseed oil and beef tallow as feedstock, the plant will have a production capacity of approximately 3,000 barrels per day. The project is targeting a Final Investment Decision (FID) in 2027, with commercial operations expected to commence in 2030. Once operational, the facility is projected to avoid over 390,000 tons of COâ‚‚ emissions annually, equivalent to removing around 90,000 petrol-powered passenger vehicles from the road.
author: Analysts Account
